scope.summary:
Integrate the native editors with the WP-011 shell quick-switcher (WP-011 MT-017 target), extending the existing native quick-switcher module (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs) that ports the React QuickSwitcher (app/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx). The quick switcher is a global keyboard-activated overlay (Ctrl+P / Cmd+P) that searches the full project graph (loom_block, file, tag_hub, document, symbol, work_packet, micro_task, user_manual_page, wiki_page) and opens the selected target in the appropriate editor or panel.

This MT has two distinct responsibilities:
(A) Implement/extend the QuickSwitcher panel (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs): the floating modal overlay with search input and result list, bound to GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search and GET/POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents. Reuse the existing WP-011 command surface (command_registry.rs + command_palette.rs) for shortcut activation and the shared selection/dispatch bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs).
(B) Wire the open-target dispatch: resolve each LoomGraphSearchHit to a NavigationTarget and dispatch it to the correct editor/panel in the shell - opening documents in the rich-text editor (MT-011 surface), loom blocks in the Loom viewer (MT-021, reuse loom_graph.rs), code symbols in the code editor (MT-001), work packets/microtasks in the GOV viewer, user manual pages, and wiki pages. This is the 'integration' part: after this MT, selecting any quick-switcher result navigates inside the native shell.

Diff shape: edit/extend src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs (~300 lines of net additions). Add a QuickSwitcher overlay entry to the shell's modal/overlay stack (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs). Register the global keybinding for Ctrl+P (or platform shortcut) through the existing command_registry.rs command surface. Add HTTP fns in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs: search_loom_graph (graph search endpoint) and list/record quick_switcher_recents.

Features to implement:

1. MODAL OVERLAY: the quick switcher is a full-screen semi-transparent overlay containing a centered card. It is activated by Ctrl+P (Cmd+P on macOS). Pressing Escape or clicking outside the card closes it. On open, the search input receives focus immediately (first-frame autofocus).

2. QUERY INPUT: single-line TextEdit, placeholder 'Open by title, block, symbol, WP, MT, manual, or wiki page'. On each keystroke, after a 150ms debounce, fire GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search with {q: trimmed, limit: 25, sourceKinds: [loom_block, file, tag_hub, document, symbol, work_packet, micro_task, user_manual_page, wiki_page]}. Cancel previous in-flight request (use a generation counter or cancel token).

3. RECENTS: on open (when workspace_id is Some), GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents?limit=20. Store recents as Vec<String> (hit_keys = '{source_kind}:{ref_id}'). When ordering results, promote recents to the top (sort: recents-first by position in the recents list, then remaining results in original order). Mirror the React orderedResults useMemo (QuickSwitcher.tsx:154-164).

4. RESULT LIST: scrollable list of LoomGraphSearchHit items. Show up to 25 items. For each hit: source_kind badge (from SOURCE_LABELS map: loom_block->'Loom Block', file->'File', tag_hub->'Tag Hub', document->'Document', symbol->'Symbol', work_packet->'Work Packet', micro_task->'Microtask', user_manual_page->'UserManual Page', wiki_page->'Wiki Page'), title, excerpt, and the open-target label (from resolve_open_target). Keyboard navigation: ArrowDown/ArrowUp moves selection; Enter activates the selected item. Mouse hover also updates selection.

5. STATUS LINE: 'Type to search the project graph' (no query), 'No workspace selected' (no workspace), 'Searching graph...' (loading), error string, or '{N} result(s)' (loaded). If recents failed to load, append '; durable recents unavailable: {error}'.

6. OPEN-TARGET DISPATCH (the integration core): implement resolve_open_target(hit: LoomGraphSearchHit) -> NavigationTarget porting the React openTargetForLoomSearchHit (app/src/lib/loom_search_open_target.ts). Navigation targets:
   - wiki_page + non-empty ref_id -> NavigationTarget::WikiPage { projection_id }
   - user_manual_page -> NavigationTarget::UserManualPage { slug } (from metadata.page_slug or ref_id)
   - document with KRD-prefixed doc id -> NavigationTarget::Document { document_id } (check metadata.rich_document_id, metadata.document_id, block.document_id)
   - loom_block with document_id -> NavigationTarget::Document { document_id } (open source doc)
   - loom_block with no document_id -> NavigationTarget::LoomBlock { block_id: ref_id }
   - file, tag_hub -> NavigationTarget::LoomBlock { block_id: ref_id }
   - symbol -> NavigationTarget::CodeSymbol { symbol_entity_id: ref_id }
   - work_packet -> NavigationTarget::WorkPacket { wp_id } (from metadata.work_packet_id, metadata.wp_id, or parsed from ref_id via WP-xxx pattern)
   - micro_task -> NavigationTarget::MicroTask { mt_id, wp_id? }
   - fallback -> NavigationTarget::None (disabled item)
   Each NavigationTarget arm dispatches to the appropriate panel via the shell's navigation bus (a channel or trait object built on the existing event_bus.rs + command_registry.rs surface): open_document(id), open_loom_block(id), open_code_symbol(id), open_work_packet(id), open_micro_task(mt_id, wp_id?), open_user_manual_page(slug), open_wiki_page(projection_id).

7. RECENT RECORDING: on successful open, POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents with body {result_kind, source_kind, ref_id, title, excerpt, metadata}. On success, prepend the returned hit_key to the local recents Vec (deduplicated, max 20). Mirror QuickSwitcher.tsx:177-187.

8. DISABLED ITEMS: if resolve_open_target returns NavigationTarget::None, render the result item as disabled (grayed, not clickable). Mirror target.enabled check in React.

Files to create/edit:
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs (existing WP-011 module; extend with graph-search, recents, resolve_open_target, dispatch)
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs (module wiring if a new submodule is added)
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs (modal overlay stack, Ctrl+P keybind registration via command_registry, navigation dispatch)
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs (register the Ctrl+P quick-switcher command if not already present)
- EDIT: src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (search_loom_graph if not already from MT-029, list_quick_switcher_recents, record_quick_switcher_recent)

Implementation steps:
1. Define QuickSwitcherState: query, results (Vec<LoomGraphSearchHit>), recents (Vec<String>), selected_index, loading, error, recents_error, debounce_generation (u64).
2. Implement search_loom_graph() in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs: GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search with params {q, limit, sourceKinds}. This is the SAME endpoint WorkspaceSearchPanel uses (verify the route). Return Vec<LoomGraphSearchHit>.
3. Implement list_quick_switcher_recents(workspace_id, limit): GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents?limit=20. Returns Vec<QuickSwitcherRecent {hit_key, ...}>.
4. Implement record_quick_switcher_recent(workspace_id, payload): POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents. Returns QuickSwitcherRecent {hit_key}.
5. Implement resolve_open_target(hit) -> NavigationTarget in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs (port of loom_search_open_target.ts).
6. Render the modal overlay: use egui::Area or a full-window egui::Window with no title bar, full-screen semi-transparent background. The card is a fixed-width (e.g. 600px), auto-height window centered. On click outside the card bounds, close.
7. Render query TextEdit with autofocus on first open (use a one-shot focus flag in state).
8. On each query change: increment debounce_generation; spawn a tokio task that sleeps 150ms, checks generation still matches (cancel token pattern), then fires the HTTP request. Return results via channel (reuse event_bus.rs for delivery if appropriate).
9. Compute ordered_results: let recent_rank = recents.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, k)| (k, i)).collect HashMap; sort results by recent_rank presence and position.
10. Render result list with keyboard navigation. Track selected_index. On ArrowDown/Up adjust index. On Enter call select_hit. On mouse hover update index.
11. On select_hit: call record_quick_switcher_recent (fire-and-forget, don't block), then dispatch resolve_open_target result through the shell navigation bus, then close the modal.
12. Register Ctrl+P (egui input: egui::Key::P + Modifiers::CTRL) as the global shortcut to toggle the quick switcher open/closed, via command_registry.rs.
13. Assign AccessKit author_ids (reuse accessibility/* infrastructure): 'quick-switcher' (overlay), 'quick-switcher.search' (input), 'quick-switcher.status' (status label), result items 'quick-switcher.result.{source_kind}.{stable_ref_id}' where stable_ref_id is ref_id with [^a-zA-Z0-9_-] replaced by '-'.

WP-011 MT-017 integration note: WP-011 MT-017 defined the quick-switcher target in the shell (app.rs) and shipped the base quick_switcher.rs module. This MT delivers the integration (graph search + dispatch). The shell navigation bus interface (trait/channel built on event_bus.rs) should be designed to be extensible so E5 interconnection MTs (MT-031 etc.) can add new dispatch targets without touching this file.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: depends on MT-028 (LoomSearchV2Surface, for on_open_loom_block being wired in the shell) and MT-029 (FindInFiles, for search_loom_graph HTTP fn potentially shared). In practice both can be parallelized if the HTTP fn is extracted first. The shell navigation bus interface must be defined (can be a stub trait with NotImplemented arms) before this MT finalises, or this MT defines it.

Risk if missed: the quick switcher is the primary keyboard-driven navigation surface used by both humans and swarm agents. Without it, agents must navigate by clicking panel buttons, which is fragile and violates HBR-SWARM. It also directly affects HBR-MAN (swarm navigability).
acceptance_criteria: QuickSwitcherState struct exists in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs with fields: query, results, recents, selected_index, loading, error, recents_error.
Ctrl+P (or the platform-appropriate shortcut registered via command_registry.rs in the shell) opens the quick switcher overlay; Escape closes it.
On open, the search input receives focus on the first rendered frame (autofocus flag).
Typing a query triggers GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search after 150ms debounce; results appear in the result list.
Recents are fetched on panel open via GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents and displayed at the top of results.
ArrowDown/ArrowUp keyboard navigation moves the selected index; Enter activates the selected item.
resolve_open_target correctly maps all 9 source_kind values to a NavigationTarget; NavigationTarget::None items are rendered as disabled.
Selecting a loom_block hit (with no document_id) calls on_open_loom_block with the correct block_id and closes the modal.
Selecting a document hit calls on_open_document with the correct KRD-prefixed document_id.
On successful selection, POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents is called and the returned hit_key is prepended to the recents list.
All AccessKit author_ids listed in implementation_notes are present in the egui_kittest snapshot (reusing accessibility/* infrastructure).
cargo build -p handshake-native succeeds with no new warnings from this MT.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native quick_switcher_search -- --nocapture: real-PG integration test. Seeds 3 LoomBlocks (types: loom_block, document, symbol). Calls GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search (or the equivalent graph-search endpoint) with q matching the seeded titles. Asserts hits.len() >= 1 with correct source_kind, title, ref_id.
cargo test -p handshake-native quick_switcher_recents_roundtrip -- --nocapture: POSTs a QuickSwitcherRecent, GETs the recents list, asserts the new hit_key appears first.

cargo test -p handshake-native quick_switcher_resolve_open_target -- --nocapture: unit tests for resolve_open_target covering all 9 source_kinds. At minimum assert: loom_block with ref_id 'BLK-1' and no document_id -> LoomBlock{block_id:'BLK-1'}; loom_block with metadata.rich_document_id='KRD-1' -> Document{document_id:'KRD-1'}; symbol with ref_id 'SYM-1' -> CodeSymbol{symbol_entity_id:'SYM-1'}; wiki_page with ref_id 'WP-1' -> WikiPage{projection_id:'WP-1'}; unknown source_kind -> None.

egui_kittest snapshot: render the quick switcher (open=true) with injected results (4 hits covering loom_block, document, symbol, work_packet), assert AccessKit tree contains 'quick-switcher', 'quick-switcher.search', 'quick-switcher.status', and at least one 'quick-switcher.result.*' node. Assert the loom_block item is not disabled and the NavigationTarget::None item is disabled.

Navigation dispatch integration test: construct the shell (app.rs) with a mock navigation bus; open the quick switcher with a seeded loom_block hit; simulate Enter; assert on_open_loom_block was called with the correct block_id and the panel is no longer open.
implementation_notes: Cross-WP dependency: this MT extends the WP-011 quick-switcher module shipped by WP-KERNEL-011 MT-017 (the shell quick-switcher target). That cross-WP relationship is prose context only and is not a depends_on entry in this MT's lifecycle.

Graph search endpoint: verify whether the quick-switcher search maps to GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/search (same as find-in-files) or a dedicated /loom/graph-search route. The React searchLoomGraph() call (QuickSwitcher.tsx:126-133) uses sourceKinds as a filter; check the backend route handler (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs around line 3394/3508) for the exact param contract. If MT-029 already implements search_loom_graph() in backend_client.rs, reuse that fn directly.

Cancel-previous-request pattern for debounce: store debounce_generation: Arc<AtomicU64> in state. On each query change, increment. In the spawned task, capture the generation at spawn time. After the 150ms sleep, compare with current; if changed, discard the response. This avoids showing stale results from a superseded query.

Modal rendering in egui: use egui::Area with Order::Foreground for the semi-transparent background, then an egui::Window with no title bar for the card. The WP-011 shell already has a modal/overlay mechanism in app.rs (and the existing quick_switcher.rs base module); use that. Do not fight the existing shell architecture; adapt to whatever modal mechanism WP-011 MT-017 specified.

Reuse the existing command surface: register the Ctrl+P activation through command_registry.rs (and surface it in command_palette.rs) rather than inventing a new input layer; there is no standalone input module.

Autofocus: set a bool focus_requested: bool in state, initialized true. On the first show() call where focus_requested is true, call ui.memory_mut(|m| m.request_focus(search_id)) and set focus_requested = false.

Ordered results: let recent_rank: HashMap<&str, usize> = recents.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, k)| (k.as_str(), i)).collect(); then sort results clone by: (recent_rank.get(hit_key(hit)), position_in_original). Recents-first, then original order.
hit_key fn: format!('{source_kind}:{ref_id}'). Mirror the React hitKey fn (QuickSwitcher.tsx:57-59).

stable_part fn for AccessKit IDs: trim, replace [^a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ with '-', strip leading/trailing '-'. If result empty, use 'item'. Mirror the React stablePart fn (QuickSwitcher.tsx:48-55). Reuse the accessibility/* registry/snapshot infrastructure for author_id assignment.

Navigation bus: define a trait ShellNavigator (or use a struct of fn pointers / tokio mpsc channels built on event_bus.rs) with methods: open_document(doc_id: String), open_loom_block(block_id: String), open_code_symbol(entity_id: String), open_work_packet(wp_id: String), open_micro_task(mt_id: String, wp_id: Option<String>), open_user_manual_page(slug: String), open_wiki_page(projection_id: String). The quick switcher receives &dyn ShellNavigator (or a clone of the channel sender). This interface will be reused by E5 interconnection MTs.

Source labels map: loom_block->'Loom Block', file->'File', tag_hub->'Tag Hub', document->'Document', symbol->'Symbol', work_packet->'Work Packet', micro_task->'Microtask', user_manual_page->'UserManual Page', wiki_page->'Wiki Page'. Match the React SOURCE_LABELS (QuickSwitcher.tsx:23-33).

work_packet_id extraction from metadata: check metadata.work_packet_id, then metadata.wp_id, then parse metadata.entity_key for WP-[A-Za-z0-9-]+ pattern, then parse ref_id for the same pattern. Mirror workPacketId fn (loom_search_open_target.ts:36-43).

micro_task_target extraction: check metadata.micro_task_id, metadata.mt_id, then parse metadata.entity_key/ref_id for MT-[A-Za-z0-9-]+ pattern. wp_id from metadata.work_packet_id, metadata.wp_id, metadata.work_packet, or metadata.entity_key WP parse. Mirror microTaskTarget fn (loom_search_open_target.ts:45-58).

Quick-switcher recents endpoint: GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents?limit=20 (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs, list_quick_switcher_recents handler). POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents (record_quick_switcher_recent handler). POST body: {result_kind, source_kind, ref_id, title, excerpt, metadata}. Response: {hit_key, ...}.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx (primary reference - full quick switcher feature)
app/src/lib/loom_search_open_target.ts (resolve_open_target logic - port entirely for NavigationTarget dispatch)
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search (or /loom/graph-search) - graph search with sourceKinds filter (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs around line 278 / 3394). Params: q, limit, sourceKinds, offset.

GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents?limit=20 - list recent hits (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs, list_quick_switcher_recents). Returns Vec<QuickSwitcherRecent {hit_key, result_kind, source_kind, ref_id, title, excerpt, metadata}>.

POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents - record a new recent hit (src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs, record_quick_switcher_recent). Body: QuickSwitcherRecentInput {result_kind, source_kind, ref_id, title, excerpt, metadata}. Response: QuickSwitcherRecent {hit_key, ...}.

risks: Navigation bus not defined by the time this MT is executed: if WP-011 MT-017 left the navigation interface as a stub, this MT must define it; failing to do so means dispatch calls cannot compile.
Debounce cancel race: if the cancel-token pattern is not implemented, a slow 300ms HTTP response from a previous query could overwrite a newer result, showing stale data.

Modal focus theft: if the egui overlay captures keyboard input globally without proper scoping, it may intercept keystrokes intended for background panels after the modal is closed, violating HBR-QUIET.

resolve_open_target exhaustiveness: if a new source_kind is added to the backend and the Rust match is non-exhaustive (missing an arm), the compiler won't catch it if using a string match; use an enum with a catch-all arm and log unknown kinds.
Recents deduplication: if the prepend-and-deduplicate logic is not implemented, the recents list grows unbounded and shows duplicate entries, breaking the ordering invariant.
NavigationTarget::None items clickable: if the disabled check is omitted, clicking a 'No direct app target yet' item will panic or no-op without feedback.

graph-search endpoint ambiguity: the quick switcher uses 'searchLoomGraph' in React which may map to a different route than the workspace search's paginated endpoint; using the wrong route will return wrong results or wrong field names.

AccessKit IDs for result rows: if the stable_part fn produces empty strings for certain ref_ids (e.g. UUIDs with hyphens only), multiple rows will have the same AccessKit ID, breaking agent targeting.
minimum_controls: Navigation bus trait must be defined (even with stub implementations) before this MT is merged; test that all 7 dispatch arms compile.
Debounce cancel test: unit test that simulates two rapid query changes and asserts only the second response is shown (first is discarded).
Modal keyboard scoping: assert in kittest that after closing the modal, a keypress on a background text editor is received by the editor and not the modal.
resolve_open_target must cover all 9 source_kinds with explicit match arms; use a catch-all that returns NavigationTarget::None with a logged warning for unknown kinds.
Recents dedup test: unit test that calling prepend_dedup with the same hit_key twice produces a list with that key appearing only once.
Disabled item rendering test: egui_kittest assert that a hit with NavigationTarget::None produces a widget with enabled=false in the AccessKit tree.
Verify graph-search route: read src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs routes section before coding to confirm the exact path and param names used by searchLoomGraph.
stable_part uniqueness: unit test that stable_part produces distinct non-empty strings for the ref_ids of the 9 hit types in the kittest snapshot.
